sections.paragraph:
ἐπιτηδεύω fut. 2 sg. ἐπιτηδεύσεις Jer 2:33; 1 aor. ἐπετήδευσα (s. prec. entry; Soph., Hdt. et al.; Diod S 1, 70, 2; Epict. 3, 1, 15; 3, 5, 8; BGU 1253, 11 [II b.c.]; LXX; En 9:6; Jos., Ant. 18, 66, C. Ap. 2, 189; Tat.; Ath., R. 48, 18 al.) to make someth. an object of one’s purpose, take care w. ὡς foll. MPol 17:1.—DELG s.v. ἐπιτηδέ.

fut. fut. = fut.

  1. sg. = singular

aor. aor. = aorist

prec. prec. = preceding

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

BGU BGU = Aegyptische Urkunden aus den Museen zu Berlin: Griechische Urkunden—List 4

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

En En = I Enoch—List 2

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

someth. someth. = something

foll. foll. = followed, following

MPol MPol = Martyrdom of Polycarp; after II a.d.—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 384
